title: Loggly Documentation
prompt_source: Inline docs prompt; CONNECTOR_DOCUMENTATION_GUIDELINES fetched via getDocGuidelines()

sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
**Loggly** (SolarWinds Loggly) is a hosted log-management SaaS. The Shielva connector — `LogglyConnector` (`CONNECTOR_TYPE = "loggly"`, `AUTH_TYPE = "api_key"`) — wraps BOTH Loggly HTTP surfaces in a single client:

| Surface | Base URL | Auth |
|---|---|---|
| Management / Search | `https://{subdomain}.loggly.com/apiv2` | HTTP Basic `username:password` |
| Bulk Send | `https://logs-01.loggly.com/bulk/{customer_token}/tag/{tag}/` | URL-embedded customer token (no header) |

Methods (each a standalone public `async def` in `connector.py`):

- Search: `search_logs`, `get_search_field_stats`
- Saved Searches: `list_saved_searches`, `create_saved_search`
- Alerts: `list_alerts`, `create_alert`
- Dashboards: `list_dashboards`, `get_dashboard`
- Source Groups: `list_source_groups`
- Users: `list_users`
- Bulk Send: `send_events_bulk`

The connector normalises events into `NormalizedDocument` (id = `f"{tenant_id}_{source_id}"`), retries 429/5xx with exponential backoff (3 attempts honoring `Retry-After` when present), and never embeds raw HTTP in `connector.py` (SOC — all HTTP delegated to `client/http_client.py::LogglyHTTPClient`).

sections.title: Quick Start
sections.content:
### 1. Gather your Loggly credentials

1. Sign in at `https://{subdomain}.loggly.com`.
2. Open **Settings → Account → Account Information** and note your **subdomain** (the prefix in the URL).
3. Either reuse your account username/password OR create an API-credential pair under **Settings → Account → API Tokens**. Both shapes are valid Basic-auth credentials.
4. For bulk send, open **Settings → Source Setup → Customer Tokens** and copy a token UUID.

### 2. Install in ACP

In **ACP → Manage Connectors → Loggly**, fill in:

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| `subdomain` (text) | e.g. `acme` |
| `username` (text) | Loggly username |
| `password` (secret) | Loggly password |
| `customer_token` (secret, optional) | UUID; required only for `send_events_bulk` |
| `base_url` (text, optional) | Override `https://{subdomain}.loggly.com/apiv2` (rare) |
| `ingest_base_url` (text, optional) | Override `https://logs-01.loggly.com` |
| `rate_limit_per_min` (number) | Soft cap, default 60 |

Click **Install**. `install()` validates `subdomain` + `username` + `password` are present (it does NOT call Loggly), initialises the `LogglyHTTPClient`, and returns `ConnectorStatus(HEALTHY, AUTHENTICATED)`.

### 3. First health check

The gateway calls `health_check()` which runs `GET /apiv2/search?q=*&size=1` as a lightweight probe. On 2xx → `HEALTHY + CONNECTED`. On 401 → `OFFLINE + TOKEN_EXPIRED`. On 403 → `UNHEALTHY + INVALID_CREDENTIALS`. On 429 → `DEGRADED + CONNECTED` with structured-log warning.

sections.title: Authentication
sections.content:
Loggly exposes two distinct auth modes that the connector handles in a single HTTP client (`AUTH_TYPE = "api_key"` is the umbrella label since both are static, non-OAuth credentials).

### Management API — HTTP Basic

Every request to `https://{subdomain}.loggly.com/apiv2/*`:

```
Authorization: Basic base64("{username}:{password}")
Content-Type:  application/json
Accept:        application/json
```

### Bulk Send — Token in URL

`send_events_bulk` posts to:

```
POST https://logs-01.loggly.com/bulk/{customer_token}/tag/{tag}/
Content-Type: application/json
Body: newline-delimited JSON (one event per line)
```

No `Authorization` header is sent — the `customer_token` UUID embedded in the path IS the credential. If `customer_token` is blank, calling `send_events_bulk` raises `LogglyError` without making a network call.

### Lifecycle

| Phase | Behaviour |
|---|---|
| `install()` | Validates `subdomain` + `username` + `password`. Initialises `LogglyHTTPClient`. Does **not** call any Loggly endpoint. |
| `authorize(...)` | Returns a stub `TokenInfo` — there is no token exchange. |
| `health_check()` | `GET /apiv2/search?q=*&size=1` — minimal probe. |
| `ensure_token()` | Not called — no token to refresh. |
| token storage | None. |

sections.title: Configuration
sections.content:
### Install fields (per-tenant)

Read via `self.config.get(...)` in `connector.py::__init__` — never hardcoded.

| Key | Type | Required | Read in code | Purpose |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `subdomain` | text | yes | `self.subdomain` | URL prefix for Management API |
| `username` | text | yes | `self.username` | Basic-auth username |
| `password` | secret | yes | `self.password` | Basic-auth password |
| `customer_token` | secret | no | `self.customer_token` | UUID in bulk-send URL path |
| `base_url` | text | no | `self.base_url` (default `https://{subdomain}.loggly.com/apiv2`) | Override Management base |
| `ingest_base_url` | text | no | `self.ingest_base_url` (default `https://logs-01.loggly.com`) | Override Bulk Send base |
| `rate_limit_per_min` | number | no | `self.rate_limit_per_min` (default 60) | Client-side soft cap |

### Class constants (provider-wide)

```python
REQUIRED_CONFIG_KEYS = ["subdomain", "username", "password"]
_STATUS_MAP = {
    401: ("OFFLINE",   "TOKEN_EXPIRED"),
    403: ("UNHEALTHY", "INVALID_CREDENTIALS"),
    429: ("DEGRADED",  "CONNECTED"),
}
_DEFAULT_INGEST_BASE = "https://logs-01.loggly.com"
```

sections.title: API Methods
sections.content: All public async methods on `LogglyConnector`. SOC: connector.py orchestrates only; HTTP lives in `client/http_client.py::LogglyHTTPClient`.

children.title: Lifecycle
children.content:
### `async install() -> ConnectorStatus`
Validate `subdomain` + `username` + `password`; initialise `LogglyHTTPClient`. Does NOT call the API.

### `async authorize(auth_code, state) -> TokenInfo`
No-op for api_key/Basic auth — returns a stub TokenInfo.

### `async health_check() -> ConnectorStatus`
Runs `GET /apiv2/search?q=*&size=1`. Classifies failures by `_STATUS_MAP`.

### `async sync(since=None, full=False, kb_id=None, webhook_url=None) -> SyncResult`
Paginates `/apiv2/events/iterate` over a 24h (or `since`-bounded) window → `NormalizedDocument` → `ingest_document`. Returns `SyncResult` with `documents_synced / _failed / _found`.

children.title: Search
children.content:
### `async search_logs(*, q='*', from_='-24h', until='now', size=100, order='desc') -> Dict`
`GET /apiv2/search?q={q}&from={from_}&until={until}&size={size}&order={order}` — returns `{rsid, events}`.

### `async get_search_field_stats(field, *, q='*', from_='-24h', until='now', facet_size=100) -> Dict`
`GET /apiv2/fields/{field}` — facet aggregation for one field across the time window.

children.title: Saved Searches
children.content:
### `async list_saved_searches() -> Dict`
`GET /apiv2/savedsearches`.

### `async create_saved_search(payload: Dict) -> Dict`
`POST /apiv2/savedsearches` — body is the saved-search JSON (`{name, query, ...}`).

children.title: Alerts
children.content:
### `async list_alerts() -> Dict`
`GET /apiv2/alerts`.

### `async create_alert(payload: Dict) -> Dict`
`POST /apiv2/alerts` — body is the alert spec (`{name, query, type, thresholdValue, timeRange, endpoints}`).

children.title: Source Groups + Users
children.content:
### `async list_source_groups() -> Dict`
`GET /apiv2/sourcegroups`.

### `async list_users() -> Dict`
`GET /apiv2/users` — requires admin role on the Loggly side.

children.title: Bulk Event Send
children.content:
### `async send_events_bulk(events: List[Dict], tag='bulk') -> Dict`
`POST https://logs-01.loggly.com/bulk/{customer_token}/tag/{tag}/`.

Body is newline-delimited JSON — one event per line, up to 5 MB total. Requires `customer_token` to be configured at install time; raises `LogglyError` otherwise. An empty `events` list short-circuits to `{response: 'ok', sent: 0}` (no HTTP call).

sections.title: Error Handling
sections.content:
Exception hierarchy in `exceptions.py`:

```
LogglyError                              # base; carries status_code + response_body
├── LogglyAuthError                      # 401 / 403
├── LogglyBadRequestError                # 400
├── LogglyNotFoundError                  # 404
├── LogglyConflictError                  # 409
├── LogglyRateLimitError                 # 429 — retry_after_s
└── LogglyServerError                    # 5xx
```

Back-compat aliases:
```
LogglyNetworkError = LogglyServerError   # legacy name
LogglyNotFound     = LogglyNotFoundError
```

### Retry behaviour (`client/http_client.py::_request`)

| Status | Action |
|---|---|
| 400 / 401 / 403 / 404 / 409 | Raise immediately |
| 429 | Honour `Retry-After` if present; otherwise exponential backoff `0.5 * 2 ** attempt` (0.5s, 1s, 2s). Up to `_MAX_RETRIES = 3` |
| 5xx | Exponential backoff, up to 3 retries |
| `httpx.TimeoutException` / `httpx.NetworkError` | Backoff, retry up to 3, then raise `LogglyServerError` |

### How errors surface to the gateway

`health_check()` catches exceptions and maps via `_STATUS_MAP`:

```
401 → ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE,   TOKEN_EXPIRED)
403 → ConnectorStatus(UNHEALTHY, INVALID_CREDENTIALS)
429 → ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED,  CONNECTED)   — logged as warning
other → ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED, CONNECTED) or (OFFLINE, CONNECTED) on transport
```

sections.title: Troubleshooting
sections.content:
| Symptom | Probable cause | Fix |
|---|---|---|
| `ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE, MISSING_CREDENTIALS)` at install | `subdomain`, `username`, or `password` blank | Re-enter all three in ACP. |
| `ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE, TOKEN_EXPIRED)` at health_check | 401 — Basic-auth credentials wrong, or `subdomain` mis-typed | Verify subdomain matches the URL you log into. Rotate password if needed. |
| `ConnectorStatus(UNHEALTHY, INVALID_CREDENTIALS)` at health_check | 403 — user lacks role required for the endpoint (e.g. `list_users` needs admin) | Promote the user OR use an admin-scoped API-credential pair. |
| `loggly.http.retry` warnings spike | Hitting 429 (Loggly per-minute cap) | The client retries honouring `Retry-After`. Lower `rate_limit_per_min` or batch fewer requests. |
| 404 on every call | Wrong `subdomain` (e.g. typo) | Confirm the prefix in `https://{subdomain}.loggly.com` matches the Account Information page. |
| `send_events_bulk` raises `LogglyError` without HTTP call | `customer_token` install_field is blank | Re-open the connector install in ACP and paste a token from **Source Setup → Customer Tokens**. |
| Bulk events visible but not queryable in UI | Tag mismatch — events pushed under a tag your Loggly source-group doesn't include | Pass `tag=` explicitly when calling `send_events_bulk(events, tag='myapp')`. |
| `ImportError: cannot import name 'LogglyNetworkError'` | Older `exceptions.py` without back-compat aliases | Already fixed — `LogglyNetworkError = LogglyServerError` alias preserved. |
